title: kitchenkit
tagline: Modular kitchen ops tools — standalone or as MCPs inside CulinaryOS.
description: kitchenkit is a suite of focused, role-specific apps for managing kitchen operations — prep tracking, recipe management, and more. Each tool works independently for operators who need just one piece, or connects as an MCP into CulinaryOS for unified hub-level orchestration.
story: Not every kitchen needs a full platform. Some just need one tight tool that works. kitchenkit was built to solve that — task-specific apps that respect the way different roles actually use software, designed from the start to snap into CulinaryOS when the operation grows.
forWho: Kitchen managers, prep cooks, and food service operators who need targeted ops tooling — whether running standalone or inside the CulinaryOS ecosystem.

detailedSpecs.architecture: pnpm monorepo managed by Turborepo. Apps and MCP modules live in separate workspaces, with shared logic extracted into reusable packages. Supabase handles the data layer. Each MCP module exposes a CulinaryOS-compatible interface for hub integration.
detailedSpecs.dataFlow: Standalone: apps/web surfaces the UI, drawing on packages/prep-engine and packages/ratio-engine for core logic, backed by Supabase. As MCP: mcp/prep-mcp and mcp/recipe-mcp connect to CulinaryOS, routing task data through the hub and into the operator dashboard.
keyPatterns: pnpm workspaces + Turborepo for monorepo orchestration
Role-scoped MCP modules — each targets a specific kitchen task domain
Shared engines (prep, ratio) consumed by both the web app and MCP layer
Standalone-first design — no CulinaryOS dependency required
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: Railway (standalone) or embedded via CulinaryOS MCP layer
